question: Develop a C++ function `rotateArrayRight` that takes a 1D integer array and its size. The function should shift all elements one position to the right, moving the last element to the first position. Demonstrate with an example.

test_cases.expected: Undefined Behavior (e.g., crash or segmentation fault)

errors: The `rotateArrayRight` function does not handle an array of size 0 gracefully. When `size` is 0, accessing `arr[size - 1]` (i.e., `arr[-1]`) leads to undefined behavior, likely a segmentation fault or crash.
